codeReview.summary: All 8 quality polish tasks were implemented correctly. Tests are well-structured with appropriate mocking patterns, custom error classes properly replace string-based matching, error state UI clearly distinguishes fetch failures from empty data, Buyer Protection assertions use specific matchers, and ListingIntelligenceBadges has comprehensive conditional rendering coverage. A few minor non-blocking items noted.

issues.description: ValidationError class is defined but has no consumers anywhere in the codebase. This is dead code. Consider removing it or using it in existing validation logic (e.g., the owned-assets or listings routes that currently return 400 errors with inline NextResponse.json calls).

The error state UI block (lines 103-118) is nearly identical to app/my/listings/page.tsx (lines 103-118). This duplicated markup (~16 lines) could be extracted into a shared ErrorState component to reduce repetition and ensure consistent error presentation across pages.

The 'Try Again' button uses window.location.reload() which does a full page reload. A more refined approach would re-invoke the fetchOwnedAssets function directly, avoiding a full navigation cycle and preserving any existing client state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Custom error classes were introduced in lib/errors.ts for business logic errors in API routes (OwnedAssetNotFoundError, InsufficientQuantityError). This establishes a pattern where API routes should use typed error classes with instanceof checks instead of string-matching error messages. AGENTS.md doesn't mention this pattern, and future workers adding new API routes with business logic errors should follow it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/errors.ts defines the classes; app/api/user/listings/route.ts lines 107,111 throw them; catch block at lines 146-157 uses instanceof checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Error state UI pattern established for account pages (My Tickets, My Listings) with distinct error vs empty state rendering. Uses an error state variable with two message paths: HTTP errors get specific message, network errors get connection-specific message. This pattern should be documented so future account pages (e.g., order history, payment methods) follow the same approach.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/my/tickets/page.tsx and app/my/listings/page.tsx both add error state with setError in fetch catch blocks and conditional rendering in JSX.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill says to use agent-browser for verification (step 5), but this quality polish feature is purely tests/code changes — worker correctly skipped browser verification since there were no interactive flows to verify. The skill procedure could note that browser verification can be skipped for test-only/refactoring features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md step 5: 'Use agent-browser to verify real user flows'. Handoff has no interactiveChecks. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true, but browser verification was skipped without noting it as a deviation.

summary: Feature passes review. All 8 quality polish tasks are implemented correctly with proper test coverage. Custom error classes replace fragile string matching, error state UI distinguishes fetch failures from empty data, Buyer Protection matchers are now specific, and ListingIntelligenceBadges has 20 comprehensive tests. Three non-blocking issues noted: unused ValidationError class, duplicated error state UI markup across pages, and window.location.reload() instead of targeted retry. No blocking issues found.
